title: TipSubmission
description: A confidential tip submitted to the Richmond County DA — the office's one real inbound transaction. Today it exists ONLY as two Contact Form 7 forms on the WordPress site (statenislandda.org/drug-tip-form/ and /scam-tip-form/), which email the office; there is no API, no machine-readable contract, and no ticket the sender can track. This schema is modeled directly from those forms' real field names (senders-name, senders-address, senders-email, senders-phone-number, name-of-suspect, suspect-description, vehicle-description, location-description, drug-tip / scam-tip-info) and generalized into one net-new WRITE object. Serves both the create surface (submit a tip) and the read-back of a submitted tip's status.

tipId.description: Server-assigned confidential tip reference. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Set by the DA's office, not the submitter.

tipType.description: Which tip channel this came from ('drug-tip-form' vs 'scam-tip-form').

tip.description: The tip itself — narcotics activity ('drug-tip') or scam details ('scam-tip-info').

suspect.description: Optional details about the subject of the tip (drug tips).

anonymous.description: Whether the submitter is withholding their identity. Tips may be fully anonymous.

sender.description: Optional submitter contact — only if the tipster chooses to identify themselves. Maps the form's 'senders-*' fields.
